content:
# Structural Intelligence P5: HashMap Optimization -- Decision Record (2026-03-29)
## What: Completed HashMap optimization in wire_pipeline_relations() and added duplicate edge prevention.
## Key Design Decisions:
1. Source index: HashMap<(&str, u32), usize> keyed on (source_file, start_line) for O(1) source chunk lookup. Uses or_insert loop (not .collect()) to match first-match semantics of the old Iterator::position() scan.
2. with_capacity(processed.len()) on both file_index and source_index to avoid rehashing.
3. Duplicate edge prevention in LiteGraphStore::relate(): check edges_connecting() before add_edge() in petgraph. Redb already deduplicates via composite key upsert.
4. The Redb write still happens before the petgraph dedup check. This is intentional: persistence-first, dedup-in-memory-only. Moving the check before the write would require taking the graph lock before the Redb transaction, increasing contention.
## Review Findings Worth Remembering:
- HashMap .collect() uses last-writer-wins semantics. When matching first-match behavior from Iterator::position(), use an or_insert loop instead.
- edges_connecting() in petgraph is O(degree of from_node), not O(1). Acceptable at current scale but could become a bottleneck on hub nodes with 100+ edges.
- test_cold_high_cosine_above_warm_low_cosine is a flaky HNSW test (approximate recall). Not related to any P1-P5 changes.
## What Blocked / Surprised:
- P3 had already partially addressed this issue by adding the file_index HashMap for target resolution. P5 only needed to add the source index and edge dedup.
